name: Fiverr (Gaming)
description: A large general freelance marketplace where gamers can buy/sell coaching, play-with-me sessions, boosting and other game-related services.

details.targetMarket: Casual and competitive gamers, streamers, and esports players seeking one-off coaching, play-with-me sessions, boosting, and game-related creative services.

items: Large category taxonomy for gaming (coaching, boosting, montages, overlays)
Gig packages and extras to productize services
Stores for themed bundles (Gamers & Streamers Store)
Escrow-style payment (buyers pay up front; sellers paid after clearance)
Rating & review system, seller levels (Top Rated, Pro)
Buyer service fee and seller commission
Built-in paid video consultations and Zoom integration for scheduled sessions
Gig-level scheduling guidance and ability to link external scheduling tools
Seller Plus subscription tiers for sellers
Promoted Gigs / Gig Ads for visibility

missingFeatures: No standardized recurring booking/subscription flow for weekly or ongoing coaching clients (coaches must use custom workarounds or external schedulers)
No verified skill/training credential layer specific to gaming (beyond generic Pro/badge and reviews) to guarantee high-ticket coaching quality
Discovery and comparison friction: pricing and deliverables vary seller-by-seller with weak standardization, making apples-to-apples comparison of live sessions difficult
Limited built-in availability filtering (timezones/real-time availability) and no built-in timezone-aware booking calendar for buyers to instantly book live gameplay sessions
Marketplace signal dilution for niche high-quality gaming coaches — large volume of low-price offers reduces visibility for premium coaches
Seller economics and payout friction (flat 20% commission + buyer fees) create margin pressure for coaches selling frequent live sessions

pricingPlans.name: Pay-per-gig (buyer)
pricingPlans.note: Primary buyer model: one-off purchases or custom offers priced by each seller; mandatory buyer service fee applied at checkout.

pricingPlans.name: Seller Plus (tiers)
pricingPlans.note: Optional subscription for sellers that adds analytics, coupons, early payout and promotional tools (exact tiers vary).

pricingPlans.name: Fiverr Pro
pricingPlans.price: Custom / premium (Pro gigs often start much higher)
pricingPlans.note: Hand-vetted professional tier for higher-trust, higher-priced services (buyers pay per gig).

pricingEvidence.label: Wikipedia (origin of $5 gigs)
pricingEvidence.claim: Fiverr takes its name from the $5 asking price attached to all tasks when the company was founded.

pricingEvidence.label: Fiverr Help Center — Paying for orders
pricingEvidence.claim: Buyers pay a standard service fee (5.5% of purchase price and an additional fixed fee on small orders) at checkout.

pricingEvidence.label: Fiverr Help Center — Your earnings page
pricingEvidence.claim: Sellers receive the order amount less Fiverr's commission (Fiverr keeps 20% of seller earnings).

companyEvidence.label: Fiverr Investor Relations — Q2 2024 results
companyEvidence.claim: Active buyers as of June 30, 2024 were 3.9 million (used to represent customer scale).

companyEvidence.label: TechCrunch — Series D funding (2015)
companyEvidence.claim: Fiverr raised $60M in Series D in 2015, bringing total funding to about $110M before IPO.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase company profile
companyEvidence.claim: Company profile with founding information and funding history.

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot — customer reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Multiple buyer and seller complaints about support responsiveness, account restrictions, and verification problems.

reviewEvidence.label: Fiverr Community / Forum discussions
reviewEvidence.claim: Sellers frequently discuss high commission complaints and payout/fee frustrations on the community and forum pages.

featureEvidence.label: Fiverr Gaming category
featureEvidence.claim: Dedicated gaming category listing coaching, play-with-me, boosting and creative gaming services.

featureEvidence.label: Fiverr Help Center — Paid video consultations
featureEvidence.claim: Fiverr supports paid, scheduled video consultations (Zoom integration) with 30/60 minute session options and calendar connection.

featureEvidence.label: Fiverr Gamers & Streamers Store
featureEvidence.claim: Fiverr supports curated stores for gaming/streaming assets, bundles and recurring storefront-like offers.

strengths: Massive two-sided marketplace with millions of buyers and many gaming-specific gigs, creating high liquidity for one-off sessions
Low-friction buyer experience (search + one-click order) and escrow-style payments reduce buyer risk for live sessions
Built-in support for paid video consultations and Zoom integration enables immediate live coaching without external tooling
Strong brand recognition and SEO which drives organic demand for gaming gigs and discoverability
Flexible gig packaging (packages, extras, custom offers) lets coaches productize sessions and upsell add-ons
weaknesses: High and flat seller commission (20%) plus buyer fees, which compress margins for coaches selling frequent live sessions
Quality inconsistency and limited verified credentialing for gaming coaches beyond reviews and Pro vetting, making trust a problem for high-ticket coaching
Customer support and dispute resolution complaints are common in public reviews, which harms trust for critical purchases
Discovery and comparison friction: buyers must evaluate each coach individually because pricing/delivery units are not standardized across sellers
Limited native recurring/retainer booking and calendar-first scheduling features for ongoing coaching relationships
